sides.label: Common Belief
sides.content: Rivers only flow when it rains.

sides.label: Reality
sides.content: Forests and groundwater slowly release water into rivers, helping them flow even during dry months.

sides.label: Common Belief
sides.content: A river is just the water flowing in a channel.

sides.label: Reality
sides.content: A river includes the channel, floodplains, groundwater, and the entire ecosystem connected to it.

sides.label: Common Belief
sides.content: Rivers naturally clean all the pollution we put into them.

sides.label: Reality
sides.content: Natural processes help break down some waste, but excessive pollution overwhelms the ecosystem.
